questions.question: In the passive voice, what happens to the object of the active sentence?

options.C: It becomes the subject of the passive sentence.

questions.explanation: The source states that the object of the active sentence is used as the subject of the passive sentence.

questions.question: What is the general verb pattern for a passive construction?
questions.explanation: The verb in the active sentence is changed into its appropriate passive form: be + en-form of the main verb.

questions.question: Identify the passive form of the Present Simple: 'Badminton ________ here every afternoon.'
questions.explanation: For Present Simple, the passive uses 'is/am/are' + the past participle.

questions.question: Choose the correct passive form for the Past Simple: 'My purse ________ yesterday.'
questions.explanation: For Past Simple, the passive uses 'was/were' + the past participle.

questions.question: Convert 'The children are playing football' to the passive voice.

options.A: Football is played by the children.
options.B: Football has been played by the children.
options.C: Football is being played by the children.
options.D: Football was being played by the children.

questions.explanation: In Present Progressive passive, 'be' auxiliary + 'being' + past participle is used.

questions.question: Identify the passive form of the Past Progressive: 'The walls of my room ________ green.'
questions.explanation: Past Progressive passive uses 'was/were being' + past participle.

questions.question: Complete the Present Perfect passive: 'The room ________.'
questions.explanation: Present Perfect passive uses 'has/have been' + past participle.

questions.question: Complete the Past Perfect passive: 'Roofs of houses ________ in the storm.'
questions.explanation: Past Perfect passive uses 'had been' + past participle.

questions.question: Which of these tense forms CANNOT be passivized?
questions.explanation: The source explicitly states that Present and Past Perfect Progressive verbs cannot be passivized.

questions.question: Identify the passive with a modal: 'The letter ________ soon.'
questions.explanation: Passive with modals follows the pattern: modal + be + past participle.

questions.question: What is the passive form of 'The work should complete by 5 p.m.'?

options.A: The work should be completed by 5 p.m.
options.B: The work is completed by 5 p.m.
options.C: The work should been completed by 5 p.m.
options.D: The work will be completed by 5 p.m.

questions.explanation: Passive modals use 'should/must/can' + be + past participle.

questions.question: Select the correct informal passive: 'They had their house ________ yellow.'
questions.explanation: Informal passives use 'have/get' + object + past participle.

questions.question: Convert to passive: 'The students have elected Mr. Das President.'

options.B: Mr. Das has been elected President.
options.C: President has been elected Mr. Das.

questions.explanation: In sentences with an object complement, the object becomes the subject and the complement stays after the verb.

questions.question: Which is a correct passive of 'Father gave the son fifty rupees'?

options.A: The son was given fifty rupees.
options.B: Fifty rupees was given to the son.
options.C: The son was being given fifty rupees.

questions.explanation: Verbs with two objects can have two passive forms, often using the nearest object as the new subject.

questions.question: Convert the reporting verb: 'People say that elephants have good memories.'

options.A: It is said that elephants have good memories.
options.B: Elephants are said to have good memories.
options.D: Elephants were said to have good memories.

questions.explanation: Reporting verbs like 'say' can be passivized using 'It is said' or by making the subordinate subject the main subject with an infinitive-.

questions.question: When the reporting verb is Present and the 'that-clause' is Past, how is it passivized?

options.B: Subject + is said + to have been ...

questions.explanation: If the main clause is present and the 'that' clause is past, we use 'It is said that...' or 'He is said to have been...'.

questions.question: Identify the passive form: 'The teacher made him stand up.'

options.B: He was made to stand up.
options.C: He made to stand up by the teacher.
options.D: Stand up was made by him.

questions.explanation: Bare infinitives (stand) in the active become to-infinitives (to stand) in the passive.

questions.question: Passive of 'I saw him climbing the ladder' is:

options.A: He was seen climbing the ladder.
options.B: He was seen to climb the ladder.
options.C: Climbing the ladder was seen by him.
options.D: He saw to be climbing the ladder.

questions.explanation: With -ing forms, the passive maintains the -ing form after the 'be + past participle'.

questions.question: Which sentence has an active form but a passive meaning?
questions.explanation: Verbs like 'need' and 'require' can take an -ing form that has a passive meaning.

questions.question: Identify the voice in Activity 1: 'Prakash killed a snake with a stick.'
questions.explanation: The subject performs the action directly.

questions.question: Identify the voice: 'Mr. Patnaik has been selected President of the Congress Party.'
questions.explanation: The auxiliary 'been' + past participle 'selected' indicates passive voice.

questions.question: Identify the voice: 'Why was he beaten?'
questions.explanation: The form 'was' + 'beaten' is passive.

questions.question: Fill the blank (Activity 2): 'English ________ all over the world.'
questions.explanation: General truth in the passive uses Present Simple passive.

questions.question: Fill the blank: 'Thousands of people ________ in the earthquake last year.'
questions.explanation: Past event requires Past Simple passive.

questions.question: Correct form: 'The accident ________ due to the driver's carelessness.'
questions.explanation: Modal 'might' followed by 'be' + past participle.

questions.question: Fill the blank: 'The road ________ when I saw it last year.'
questions.explanation: The action was in progress in the past, requiring Past Progressive passive.

questions.question: Activity 4 Newspaper report: 'Fifty houses ________ to ashes last Sunday.'
questions.explanation: Past Simple passive for a completed past action.

questions.question: Newspaper report: 'Ten children and six women ________ alive.'
questions.explanation: Agreement with plural subject in Past Simple passive.

questions.question: Newspaper report: 'About eighty people ________ for treatment.'
questions.explanation: Passive construction for receiving treatment.

questions.question: Change to passive: 'Did the hunter kill the bird yesterday?'

options.A: Was the bird killed by the hunter yesterday?
options.B: The bird was killed by the hunter yesterday?
options.C: Was the hunter killed the bird yesterday?

questions.explanation: To change a Yes/No question, first convert it to a statement, then passivize, then revert to question form-.

questions.question: Change to passive: 'Who wrote this book?'

options.A: By whom was this book written?
options.B: Who was this book written by?
options.C: This book was written by who?

questions.explanation: Questions starting with 'who' can be passivized using 'By whom' at the start or 'who' at the start with 'by' at the end.

questions.question: Which of these sentences is UNGRAMMATICAL?

options.B: A car is had by me.
options.D: The car was driven by me.

questions.explanation: The verb 'have' (meaning to possess) cannot be passivized.

questions.question: Which active verb can have a corresponding passive form?
questions.explanation: Verbs like 'resemble', 'fit', and 'lack' cannot be passivized-.

questions.question: Is 'Rama Chandra married Sita' acceptable in passive voice?

options.A: Yes: Sita was married by Rama Chandra.
options.B: No: It is absurd and unnatural.

questions.explanation: The source lists this sentence as one that has no corresponding passive form because it sounds absurd.

questions.question: Can reflexives (himself/herself) be the subject of a passive construction?
questions.explanation: Reflexives as subjects of passive constructions are unacceptable.

questions.question: Which is INCORRECT?

options.B: One another was loved by the children.

questions.explanation: Reciprocal pronouns like 'one another' cannot be the subject of a passive construction.

questions.question: Do intransitive verbs like 'sleep' or 'last' have passive forms?
questions.explanation: Intransitive and link verbs have no passive forms.

questions.question: Passive of 'Shut the door' using 'let' is:

options.D: You are ordered to shut the door.

questions.explanation: Imperative sentences can be passivized using 'let' + object + be + past participle.

questions.question: Which verb is used ONLY in the passive in these examples?
questions.explanation: The verb 'born' is used only in the passive (e.g., Gandhiji was born...).

questions.question: Can 'The car broke down' be passivized?
questions.explanation: Intransitive phrasal verbs like 'break down' cannot be passivized.

questions.question: Can 'Have the factory workers called off the strike?' be passivized?
questions.explanation: Transitive phrasal verbs like 'call off' can have passive forms.

questions.question: When do we use the 'by + agent' phrase?

options.B: When the agent is a definite/famous person or provide new information.

questions.explanation: We use it for famous authors/inventors or when it provides readers with important new information-.

questions.question: Choose the passive for (Activity 5): 'He did not leave the examination hall.' (ask to leave)
questions.explanation: The situational context suggests he wasn't asked to leave.

questions.question: Passive for (Activity 6): 'You want to talk about the Odia Bhagabat and mention its author Jagannath Das.'

options.A: The Odia Bhagabat is written by Jagannath Das.
options.B: The Odia Bhagabat was written by Jagannath Das.
options.C: Jagannath Das wrote the Odia Bhagabat.
options.D: The Odia Bhagabat had been written by Jagannath Das.

questions.explanation: Correct passive form for historical authorship.

questions.question: Fill the blank (Activity 7): 'Cheese ________ from milk.'
questions.explanation: General scientific/factual passive.

questions.question: Activity 7: 'The letter ________ a week ago.'
questions.explanation: Past Simple passive for 'a week ago'.

questions.question: Sample Question 42: 'My dog is being taken to the garden.' Identify the tense/form.
questions.explanation: The form 'is being + en' indicates Present Progressive passive.

questions.question: Sample Question 46: 'My shirt needs ironing.' Choose the passive equivalent.

options.B: My shirt needs to be ironed.
options.C: My shirt needed to be ironed.
options.D: My shirt is to need ironing.

questions.explanation: Verbs like 'need' followed by -ing can be expressed as 'need + to be + en',.

questions.question: Model Paper Question: 'How did you work out the sum?' Choose the passive voice.

options.A: How was the sum worked out?
options.B: How the sum was worked out?
options.C: How is the sum worked out?
options.D: The sum was worked out by you.

questions.explanation: Interrogative passive for Past Simple,.

questions.question: Model Paper Question: 'The businessman did not die. He ________ (murder).'
questions.explanation: Completing a situational context in Past Simple passive.

questions.question: Which verb is used in Activity 2: 'English ________ all over the world'?
questions.explanation: Correct past participle of 'speak' is 'spoken'.

questions.question: Activity 2: 'I like ________ alone.'
questions.explanation: Passive infinitive after 'like'.

questions.question: In 'It had been nicely decorated', what is the tense?
questions.explanation: 'had been + en' is the Past Perfect passive.

questions.question: What is the passive form of 'The teacher taught us geography'?
questions.explanation: Ditransitive verbs (two objects) allow both forms.

questions.question: Identify the finite verb: 'He teaches us English.'
questions.explanation: The first word in a verb phrase that takes tense is the finite verb-.

questions.question: Convert to passive: 'The teacher is working out the sum.'

options.B: The sum was being worked out.
options.C: The sum is being worked out.
options.D: The sum has been worked out.

questions.explanation: Present Progressive active to passive conversion.

questions.question: Change 'The reporter took down the speech' to passive.

options.C: The speech had been taken down.
options.D: The speech was being taken down.

questions.explanation: Past simple passive for a transitive phrasal verb,.

questions.question: Passive of 'He will help you':

options.A: You will be helped by him.
options.C: You would be helped by him.

questions.explanation: Future simple active uses 'will be + past participle' in passive.

questions.question: Is 'The meeting lasted two hours' possible in passive?
questions.explanation: Intransitive verbs like 'last' cannot be passivized.

questions.question: Passive of 'When did he return the book?'
questions.explanation: WH-question conversion to passive.

questions.question: Passive of 'Where did he find it?'
questions.explanation: Past Simple interrogative passive.

questions.question: Which of these expresses a 'state' rather than a passive action?

options.A: The letter was typed by the clerk.
options.B: She was married when I saw her.
options.C: The cake was made by mother.
options.D: The book was written by him.

questions.explanation: Past participle forms referring to a state (adjectival meaning) are not passive voice.

questions.question: In 'There is some homework to be done', 'to be done' is a:
questions.explanation: Sentences starting with 'there' can use either active or passive infinitives.

questions.question: Convert 'I have finished my work' to passive.
questions.explanation: Present Perfect passive.

questions.question: Convert 'The unique attempt was made by Janaki' to active.

options.C: Janaki has made this unique attempt.
options.D: Janaki was making this unique attempt.

questions.explanation: Reverting Past Simple passive back to active-.

questions.question: Can 'I eat rice' be naturally passivized?

options.A: Yes: Rice is eaten by me.
options.B: No: The passive form is absurd and unnatural.

questions.explanation: The source states that sentences like 'We eat rice' or 'Babies drink milk' have passive forms that are absurd.

questions.question: Passive of 'They found him dead':
questions.explanation: The object complement 'dead' remains after the verb,.

questions.question: In 'The sum is being worked out', 'being' indicates:
questions.explanation: 'being' is used in passive progressive constructions.

questions.question: Passive of 'He has not been able to overcome his fears' (Activity 9):

options.A: His fears have not been able to be overcome.
options.C: He was not able to overcome.

questions.explanation: Based on the phrasal verb exercise and passive logic for perfect tenses,.

questions.question: Which preposition is typically used in the agent phrase?
questions.explanation: The 'by + agent' phrase indicates who performed the action.

questions.question: Identify the passive voice marker in 'Thousands of people were killed'.
questions.explanation: The 'be' auxiliary and past participle together indicate passive voice.

questions.question: Convert 'Marconi invented Radio' to passive.

options.D: Radio had been invented by Marconi.

questions.explanation: Past Simple passive for definite agents.

questions.question: Change 'The postman gave me a parcel' to passive starting with 'A parcel'.

options.A: A parcel was given me by the postman.
options.B: A parcel was given to me by the postman.
options.C: A parcel was being given to me.

questions.explanation: Passive of ditransitive verb using the direct object as subject,.

questions.question: Which auxiliary is used for Present Perfect passive?
questions.explanation: 'has/have been' + past participle.

questions.question: Passive of 'He should punish for telling a lie' (Activity 2):
questions.explanation: Passive modal pattern.

questions.question: Identify the passive form in Activity 4: 'All the belongings ________ completely destroy.'
questions.explanation: Plural subject 'belongings' in Past Simple passive.

questions.question: In 'The sum is being worked out', 'is' matches the tense of:
questions.explanation: The tense of the 'be' auxiliary in both active and passive sentences is the same.

questions.question: Which verb form follows 'being' in a passive construction?
questions.explanation: In progressive passives, 'being' is followed by the past participle (en-form).

questions.question: Passive for 'He got his hair cut' (Activity 5):
questions.explanation: General passive transformation for an informal 'get' construction.

questions.question: Convert to passive: 'The unique attempt was made (by Janaki)'. What was the active subject?
questions.explanation: In 'Janaki made this unique attempt', Janaki is the active subject-.
